summary: Moved most of sysdolphin baselib gobjproc from hand-written assembly into C, deleting 262 asm lines and adding a new src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjproc.c with matched implementations for process unlink/removal, allocation, free/defer-free, and freeing all procs on a GObj. The PR body notes that only func_8038FAA8 remained unmatched. Header updates corrected func_8038FD54 to return HSD_GObjProc* and take a u8 priority, while gobj.h added clarifying comments for gobj as owner and callback as on_invoke.
what_changed: Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjproc.c with C implementations for func_8038FC18, func_8038FCE4, func_8038FD54, func_8038FE24, and func_8038FED4.
Removed the corresponding matched assembly block from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjproc.s, leaving the PR body's stated remaining unmatched function func_8038FAA8.
Updated obj_files.mk to build $(BUILD_DIR)/src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjproc.c.o alongside the remaining asm gobjproc object.
Changed the declaration of func_8038FD54 in include/functions.h from void return and s32 priority to HSD_GObjProc* return and u8 priority.
Annotated HSD_GObjProc fields in include/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h: gobj is the owner and callback is on_invoke.
Preserved external linkage to existing baselib globals such as gobjproc_alloc_data, lbl_804D7830, lbl_804D7838, lbl_804D7840, and lbl_804D7844 rather than renaming them in this slice.
smart_moves: Used exact struct field access on HSD_GObjProc and HSD_GObj instead of opaque offset math, which makes the linked-list behavior in gobjproc easier to audit while still matching.
Kept func_8038FAA8 as an external call rather than forcing an incomplete match; this allowed most of the file to move to C while leaving the known remaining hard function isolated.
Matched allocation error handling with a small inline assertProc wrapper around assert_line(31, gproc), preserving the source-line-sensitive assert shape visible in the original assembly.
Corrected func_8038FD54's prototype to reflect observed behavior: it returns the allocated HSD_GObjProc* and the priority is byte-sized.
Represented the process priority bucket lookup directly as temp_r7 + temp_r8 * (HSD_GObjLibInitData.p_link_max + 1), making the global table indexing logic explicit.
Handled deletion during traversal by saving next = cur->child before calling func_8038FE24 in func_8038FED4.
decomp_lessons: When matching baselib linked-list removal code, name temporary values after their semantic fields only when field evidence is clear; this PR used temp_r7 for gobj->p_link and temp_r8 for gproc->s_link, avoiding overclaiming stronger names.

For functions that remove nodes from multiple linked structures, match the exact order: update current-invocation globals, update priority tail/cache table, splice prev/next list links, then update child lists.
Source-line-sensitive asserts may require helper inlines to reproduce the original call shape and line numbers.
A partially matched asm file can coexist with a new C object in obj_files.mk; delete only the matched assembly region and keep the remaining asm function in place.
Function prototypes should be revised as matching reveals return values and narrower parameter types; here the allocator returns HSD_GObjProc* and priority is u8.
Bitfield struct definitions can express rlwimi/rlwinm flag manipulation cleanly when offsets and bit widths are already established.
naming_conventions: HSD_GObjProc is used as the process struct type for gobjproc routines.
HSD_GObjProc::gobj was commented as the owner field.
The allocation data global is named gobjproc_alloc_data.
The inline assertion helper was named assertProc.
Temporary names temp_r7 and temp_r8 mirror register-derived decomp naming where stronger semantic names were not committed.
Unidentified globals retained lbl_804D7830, lbl_804D7838, lbl_804D7840, and lbl_804D7844 names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted matched assembly from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjproc.s while adding the C object to TEXT_O_FILES, leaving remaining unmatched assembly available.
Used an inline assertProc wrapper to match the __assert/assert_line line-number pattern from the original assembly.
Declared unused stack storage as s32 unused[2] in func_8038FD54, likely to preserve stack layout/register allocation for matching.
Used u8 pri and bitfield assignments for flags_1, flags_2, and flags_3 to match byte stores and bit insertions at HSD_GObjProc offset 0x0D.
Matched deferred free semantics by setting lbl_804CE3E4.b2 when attempting to free the currently invoked proc under the active execution state, instead of immediately unlinking/freeing.
Used explicit null checks and linked-list traversal loops to reproduce the original branch structure for unlinking proc nodes.
follow_up_queries: Find the later PR that matches the remaining func_8038FAA8 in sysdolphin/baselib/gobjproc.
Search for later renames of lbl_804D7830, lbl_804D7838, lbl_804D7840, lbl_804D7844, and lbl_804CE3E4 to recover semantic global names.
Check subsequent gobj.h changes for finalized HSD_GObjProc field names replacing owner/on_invoke comments.
Compare later baselib gobjproc.c revisions for removal of temp_r7/temp_r8 and assertProc once better names or matching strategies were found.
